headline: Big consulting firms are going all-in on AI—and it's reshaping how they deliver value to clients

stories.title: Accenture Completes Faculty Acquisition, Expands AI Muscle on March 16
stories.summary: Accenture closed the deal on UK-based AI firm Faculty on March 16, bringing 400+ AI-native pros into the fold and appointing their CEO as CTO. This move signals Accenture's serious commitment to scaling safe, production-ready AI for enterprise clients.
stories.takeaway: Acquisition adds deep technical AI talent; sets up Accenture as a credible partner for orgs moving beyond AI pilots to real operational impact.

stories.title: Accenture Deepens Databricks Partnership on March 17 to Scale AI from Pilot to Production
stories.summary: Accenture and Databricks launched a new business group (Accenture Databricks) to help enterprises adopt Databricks as their core data and AI platform. Firms are basically saying: we'll help you move AI from experiments to real revenue-moving systems.
stories.takeaway: Partnership targets the "last mile" problem—most orgs struggle scaling AI beyond POCs; this combo gives consulting + data infra credibility.

stories.title: West Monroe Lands Forbes AI Consulting Recognition Despite Broader Consulting Consolidation Trend
stories.summary: West Monroe got named to Forbes' Best Management Consulting Firms 2026 for AI, fr one of only 37 firms hitting that category. They've embedded AI across literally 100% of their consulting delivery—no cherry-picked projects, it's everywhere.
stories.takeaway: Mid-market consulting firm is flexing AI maturity; lowkey signals that boutique/specialized firms can compete with MBB by going hard on AI-native delivery models.

watchNext.title: Capgemini: 'Death of Consulting' Narrative is Overblown—Boards Still Trust Humans Over ChatGPT
watchNext.why: Strategy chief makes the case that AI isn't replacing consultants, it's amplifying them. Tbh important pushback on the "consultants are cooked" narrative dominating headlines.

watchNext.title: PwC & Industry Signal: AI Adoption is Now Non-Negotiable for Consultant Careers
watchNext.why: PwC's explicit warning that non-adopters won't have a future at the firm is hitting different—signals that AI fluency is shifting from competitive advantage to table stakes across the industry.
